questions.text: The SI unit of electric potential is:
questions.explanation: Electric potential is measured in Volts (V). 1 Volt = 1 Joule/Coulomb.

questions.text: The capacitance of a parallel plate capacitor is given by:
questions.explanation: For a parallel plate capacitor: C = ε0A/d, where A is plate area, d is separation, ε0 is permittivity of free space.

questions.text: Equipotential surfaces are always perpendicular to electric field lines.
questions.explanation: Electric field lines are always perpendicular to equipotential surfaces because no work is done moving a charge along an equipotential.

questions.text: The energy stored in a capacitor is given by ________.
questions.explanation: Energy stored in a capacitor: U = (1/2)CV^2 = (1/2)QV = Q^2/(2C), where C is capacitance, V is potential difference, Q is charge.
